scenario_results.notes: Intake guidance requested the minimum event metadata needed for routing and avoided unsupported severity claims.
Deduplication and suppression recommendations stayed at the process level and did not drift into tool implementation.
Event-to-incident promotion used business impact and workflow state explicitly.
Escalation guidance improved queue ownership, acknowledgement, and communication quality.
Priority handling resisted title-driven pressure and stayed impact-based.
Maintenance-window advice preserved residual event visibility and explicit triggers.
Recurring-event handling preserved live event workflow while routing trend ownership to problem management.
Cross-tool conflict handling requested correlation evidence and did not invent a final system state.
Low-confidence handling requested missing service context and escalated instead of guessing impact.
Implementation work was rejected clearly and routed to adjacent specialists.
Major-incident thresholds triggered clean handoff rather than crisis-command overreach.
Fallback behavior preserved the local baseline and blocked unsupported validated claims.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against the issue #117 requirements, including normal event guidance, workflow quality, service-impact prioritization, edge cases, out-of-scope rejection, low-confidence escalation, and shared-meta-agent fallback.
Acceptance threshold is satisfied with 12 of 12 scenarios passing and no unreviewed regressions recorded.
Results reflect specialist-owned event-management deltas plus documented contracts for delegated shared-meta-agent work.
